title: viburnum opulus subsp. trilobum
intro: Viburnum opulus subsp. trilobum, commonly known as the guelder rose or European cranberrybush, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Viburnum opulus subsp. trilobum include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and tinctures, are traditionally used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
